data.content:
### Read Bank Statement and Reconciled Transactions

Source: https://developer.xero.com/documentation/guides/oauth2/scopes

Allows read-only access to bank statements and reconciled transactions. This scope is for detailed analysis of bank activity and reconciliation.

```Xero API
finance.bankstatementsplus.read
```

--------------------------------

### Read Bank Statement and Reconciliation Data

Source: https://developer.xero.com/documentation/guides/oauth2/scopes

Enables read-only access to bank statement and reconciliation data. This scope is for analyzing cash flow and reconciliation status.

```Xero API
finance.cashvalidation.read
```

--------------------------------

### Manage Bank Feeds

Source: https://developer.xero.com/documentation/guides/oauth2/scopes

Allows viewing and managing bank statement feeds within Xero. This includes managing feed connections and retrieving statement data.

```Xero API
bankfeeds
```

--------------------------------

### Retrieve Bank Transactions (GET)

Source: https://developer.xero.com/documentation/api/accounting/banktransactions

This snippet demonstrates how to retrieve bank transactions using the Xero API. It shows a basic GET request to the BankTransactions endpoint. The response includes details about each transaction, such as type, contact, line items, bank account, and reconciliation status. It also outlines optional parameters for filtering, ordering, and pagination to refine the results.

```http
GET https://api.xero.com/api.xro/2.0/BankTransactions

--Optional Parameters--
GET https://api.xero.com/api.xro/2.0/BankTransactions?where=Type%3D%3D%22SPEND%22
GET https://api.xero.com/api.xro/2.0/BankTransactions?order=Date%20DESC
GET https://api.xero.com/api.xro/2.0/BankTransactions?page=1&pageSize=100
GET https://api.xero.com/api.xro/2.0/BankTransactions/297c2dc5-cc47-4afd-8ec8-74990b8761e9

--Header Example--
If-Modified-Since: Tue, 15 Nov 2016 08:00:00 GMT
```

--------------------------------

### Retrieve Bank Transfer History

Source: https://developer.xero.com/documentation/api/accounting/banktransfers

Fetches a summary of actions performed by users on bank transfers. This functionality is accessed via the bank transactions endpoint and requires a bank transfer GUID. The response includes details about changes, dates, users, and specific actions.

```REST
GET https://api.xero.com/api.xro/2.0/BankTransactions/{Guid}/History
```

--------------------------------

### Add Note to Bank Transfer

Source: https://developer.xero.com/documentation/api/accounting/banktransfers

Allows adding a new note to a bank transfer, which will be recorded in its history. This operation is performed using the bank transactions endpoint and requires the bank transfer GUID. The request body contains the details of the note to be added.

```REST
PUT https://api.xero.com/api.xro/2.0/BankTransactions/{Guid}/History
```

--------------------------------

### Retrieve Single Bank Transfer

Source: https://developer.xero.com/documentation/api/accounting/banktransfers

Retrieves a specific bank transfer by appending its unique BankTransferID to the endpoint URL. This allows for targeted data retrieval of individual transfer records.

```http
GET https://api.xero.com/api.xro/2.0/BankTransfers/f589fb5e-34b3-4392-8207-4ba5a093ea6e
```

--------------------------------

### Retrieve or Create Bank Transfers (Xero API)

Source: https://developer.xero.com/documentation/api/accounting/banktransfers

This snippet demonstrates how to interact with the Xero Bank Transfers API to either retrieve existing bank transfers or create new ones. It supports GET and PUT methods.

```HTTP
GET https://api.xero.com/api.xro/2.0/BankTransfers
PUT https://api.xero.com/api.xro/2.0/BankTransfers
```

--------------------------------

### Retrieve All Bank Transfers

Source: https://developer.xero.com/documentation/api/accounting/banktransfers

Fetches a list of all bank transfers. This endpoint can be filtered by specifying a BankTransferID in the URL. The 'If-Modified-Since' header can be used for conditional requests based on modification time.

```http
GET https://api.xero.com/api.xro/2.0/BankTransfers
```

--------------------------------

### Upload Attachment to Bank Transfer

Source: https://developer.xero.com/documentation/api/accounting/banktransfers

Uploads an attachment to an existing bank transfer. The BankTransferID is used to construct the URL. Supports up to 10 attachments per transfer, each up to 25MB.

```http
POST /api.xro/2.0/BankTransfers/f0ec0d8c-4330-bb3b-83062c6fd8/Attachments/Image002932.png
```

--------------------------------

### Create Bank Transfer

Source: https://developer.xero.com/documentation/api/accounting/banktransfers

Creates a new bank transfer between two accounts. Requires 'FromBankAccount', 'ToBankAccount', and 'Amount'. Optional fields include 'Date', 'FromIsReconciled', 'ToIsReconciled', and 'Reference'. Transfers cannot be between accounts in different currencies.

```http
PUT https://api.xero.com/api.xro/2.0/BankTransfers
```

--------------------------------

### Retrieve Bank Transaction History (REST)

Source: https://developer.xero.com/documentation/api/accounting/banktransactions

Fetches a history of actions performed by users on a specific bank transaction. This requires the bank transaction's GUID. The response includes details about changes, dates, users, and specific actions.

```REST
GET https://api.xero.com/api.xro/2.0/BankTransactions/{Guid}/History
```

--------------------------------

### Bank Transfer History Response

Source: https://developer.xero.com/documentation/api/accounting/banktransfers

Example JSON structure for the history records of a bank transfer. It lists changes, timestamps, user information, and details of each historical event.

```JSON
{
"HistoryRecords": [
     {
"Changes": "Updated",
"DateUTCString": "2018-02-28T21:02:11",
"DateUTC": "\/Date(1519851731990+0000)\/",
"User": "System Generated",
"Details": "Received through the Xero API from ABC Org"    },
    {
"Changes": "Created",
"DateUTCString": "2018-02-28T21:01:29",
"DateUTC": "\/Date(1519851689297+0000)\/",
"User": "Mac Haag",
"Details": "INV-0041 to ABC Furniture for 100.00."    }
    ...
  ]
}
```

--------------------------------

### Bank Transfer Response Structure

Source: https://developer.xero.com/documentation/api/accounting/banktransfers

Example JSON structure for a bank transfer response, detailing fields such as BankTransferID, dates, account information, amount, and reference. It also includes details about the source and destination bank accounts.

```json
{
"BankTransfers": [
    ...
    {
"BankTransferID": "d79f3e07-5f11-45e4-9d1a-30be536d0e13",
"CreatedDateUTCString": "2018-02-15T04:22:42",
"CreatedDateUTC": "\/Date(1518668562850+0000)\/",
"DateString": "2018-01-25T00:00:00",
"Date": "\/Date(1516838400000+0000)\/",
"FromBankAccount": {
"AccountID": "ac993f75-035b-433c-82e0-7b7a2d40802c",
"Name": "Business Bank Account"      },
"ToBankAccount": {
"AccountID": "ebd06280-af70-4bed-97c6-7451a454ad85",
"Name": "Business Savings Account"      },
"Amount": "20.00",
"FromBankTransactionID": "b11794bc-775b-4f78-9b28-8f13240082ff",
"ToBankTransactionID": "f589fb5e-34b3-4392-8207-4ba5a093eae",
"FromIsReconciled": "true",
"ToIsReconciled": "true",
"Reference": "Sub 098801"    }
  ...  ]
}
```

--------------------------------

### Upload Attachment to Bank Transaction

Source: https://developer.xero.com/documentation/api/accounting/banktransactions

Example of uploading an attachment to a bank transaction. Requires the BankTransactionID and specifies content type and length in headers.

```HTTP
POST https://api.xero.com/api.xro/2.0/BankTransactions/f0ec0d8c-4330-bb3b-83062c6fd8/Attachments/Image002932.png
```

```JSON
Headers:
Authorization: Bearer...
Content Type: image/png
Content-Length: 10293Body:
{RAW-IMAGE-CONTENT}
```

--------------------------------

### Create Bank Transfer Request Body

Source: https://developer.xero.com/documentation/api/accounting/banktransfers

JSON payload for creating a bank transfer. Specifies the source and destination bank accounts using their 'Code' and the transfer 'Amount'.

```json
{
"BankTransfers": [{
"FromBankAccount": { "Code": "090" },
"ToBankAccount": { "Code": "091" },
"Amount": 20.00  }]
}
```

--------------------------------

### Xero Bank Transactions API - GET, PUT, POST Methods

Source: https://developer.xero.com/documentation/api/accounting/banktransactions

The Xero Bank Transactions API supports GET, PUT, and POST methods for managing bank transactions. These methods allow for retrieving, creating, or updating spend or receive money transactions, as well as creating overpayments and prepayments. File attachments and history retrieval are also supported.

```HTTP
GET https://api.xero.com/api.xro/2.0/BankTransactions
PUT https://api.xero.com/api.xro/2.0/BankTransactions
POST https://api.xero.com/api.xro/2.0/BankTransactions
```

--------------------------------

### Create and Retrieve Batch Payments (Xero API)

Source: https://developer.xero.com/documentation/api/accounting/batchpayments

This snippet demonstrates how to interact with the Xero Batch Payments API to create and retrieve batch payment transactions. It supports GET, PUT, and POST methods. Batch payments allow bundling multiple invoices or bills into a single payment for simplified bank reconciliation.

```HTTP
GET https://api.xero.com/api.xro/2.0/BatchPayments
PUT https://api.xero.com/api.xro/2.0/BatchPayments
POST https://api.xero.com/api.xro/2.0/BatchPayments
```

--------------------------------

### Add Note to Bank Transaction (REST)

Source: https://developer.xero.com/documentation/api/accounting/banktransactions

Adds a new note to a bank transaction, which will be recorded in its history. This operation uses a PUT request and requires the bank transaction's GUID. The note details are provided in the request body.

```REST
PUT https://api.xero.com/api.xro/2.0/BankTransactions/{Guid}/History
```

--------------------------------

### Retrieve Bank Transaction History Response (JSON)

Source: https://developer.xero.com/documentation/api/accounting/banktransactions

Example JSON response structure for retrieving bank transaction history. It contains an array of 'HistoryRecords', each detailing changes, timestamps, user information, and specific event details.

```JSON
{
"HistoryRecords": [
     {
"Changes": "Updated",
"DateUTCString": "2018-02-28T21:02:11",
"DateUTC": "\/Date(1519851731990+0000)\/",
"User": "System Generated",
"Details": "Received through the Xero API from ABC Org"    },
    {
"Changes": "Created",
"DateUTCString": "2018-02-28T21:01:29",
"DateUTC": "\/Date(1519851689297+0000)\/",
"User": "Mac Haag",
"Details": "INV-0041 to ABC Furniture for 100.00."    }
    ...
  ]
}
```

--------------------------------

### Update Bank Transaction (Spend Money)

Source: https://developer.xero.com/documentation/api/accounting/banktransactions

Example of updating an existing 'Spend Money' bank transaction. Existing line items can be updated by providing their LineItemID. New line items can be added without a LineItemID.

```JSON
{
  "Type": "SPEND",
  "Contact": {
    "ContactID": "3fa85f64-5717-4562-b3fc-2c963f66afa6"
  },
  "LineItems": [
    {
      "LineItemID": "a1b2c3d4-e5f6-7890-1234-567890abcdef",
      "Description": "Updated Office Supplies",
      "Quantity": 2,
      "UnitAmount": 60.00,
      "AccountCode": "495"
    },
    {
      "Description": "New Stationery",
      "Quantity": 5,
      "UnitAmount": 10.00,
      "AccountCode": "497"
    }
  ],
  "BankAccount": {
    "AccountID": "f44718c1-736c-4303-a719-204102207177"
  }
}
```

--------------------------------

### Create Bank Transaction with Optional Fields

Source: https://developer.xero.com/documentation/api/accounting/banktransactions

Example of creating a bank transaction including optional fields like Date, Reference, CurrencyCode, and CurrencyRate.

```JSON
{
  "Type": "RECEIVE",
  "Contact": {
    "ContactID": "3fa85f64-5717-4562-b3fc-2c963f66afa6"
  },
  "LineItems": [
    {
      "Description": "Sales Revenue",
      "UnitAmount": 100.00,
      "AccountCode": "200",
      "TaxType": "OUTPUT25"
    }
  ],
  "BankAccount": {
    "AccountID": "f44718c1-736c-4303-a719-204102207177"
  },
  "Date": "2023-10-27",
  "Reference": "INV-001",
  "CurrencyCode": "USD",
  "CurrencyRate": 0.75
}
```

--------------------------------

### Create Receive Prepayment Transaction

Source: https://developer.xero.com/documentation/api/accounting/banktransactions

Example of creating a receive prepayment transaction. This allows for multiple line items for prepayments.

```HTTP
POST https://api.xero.com/api.xro/2.0/BankTransactions
```

```JSON
{
"Type": "RECEIVE-PREPAYMENT",
"Contact": { "ContactID": "6d42f03b-181f-43e3-93fb-2025c012de92" },
"BankAccount": { "Code": "090" },
"LineAmountTypes": "Exclusive",
"LineItems": [{
"Description": "Prepayment for Kitchen Designs",
"Quantity": "1",
"UnitAmount": "500.00",
"AccountCode": "200"  },{
"Description": "Prepayment for Kitchen materials",
"Quantity": "1",
"UnitAmount": "1000.00",
"AccountCode": "200"  }]
}
```

--------------------------------

### Add Note to Bank Transaction Request (JSON)

Source: https://developer.xero.com/documentation/api/accounting/banktransactions

Example JSON structure for adding a note to a bank transaction. The 'HistoryRecords' array contains objects with a 'Details' field for the note content.

```JSON
{
"HistoryRecords": [
    {
"Details": "Note added by your favourite app!"    }
    ...
  ]
}
```

--------------------------------

### Create Receive Money Transaction with Item Code

Source: https://developer.xero.com/documentation/api/accounting/banktransactions

Example of a receive money transaction using an item code. This demonstrates how to specify an item code for a transaction.

```HTTP
POST https://api.xero.com/api.xro/2.0/BankTransactions
```

```JSON
{
"Type": "RECEIVE",
"Contact": {
"ContactID": "6d42f03b-181f-43e3-93fb-2025c012de92"
},
"Date": "2014-05-26T00:00:00",
"LineAmountTypes": "Exclusive",
"LineItems": [{
"Description": "Golf balls - white single",
"Quantity": "5",
"ItemCode": "GB1-White"
}],
"BankAccount": {
"Code": "090"
}
}
```

--------------------------------

### Delete Bank Transaction

Source: https://developer.xero.com/documentation/api/accounting/banktransactions

Example of deleting a BankTransaction by updating its Status to DELETED. Deletes are not supported for prepayment or overpayment transactions.

```HTTP
POST https://api.xero.com/api.xro/2.0/BankTransactions/4af93d54-0d13-459a-9d8a-d570982e2fb2
```

```JSON
{
"BankTransactionID": "4af93d54-0d13-459a-9d8a-d570982e2fb2",
"Status": "DELETED"
}
```

--------------------------------

### Create Receive Overpayment Transaction

Source: https://developer.xero.com/documentation/api/accounting/banktransactions

Example of creating a receive overpayment transaction. Overpayments can only have a single line item.

```HTTP
POST https://api.xero.com/api.xro/2.0/BankTransactions
```

```JSON
{
"Type": "RECEIVE-OVERPAYMENT",
"Contact": { "ContactID": "6d42f03b-181f-43e3-93fb-2025c012de92" },
"BankAccount": { "Code": "090" },
"LineAmountTypes": "NoTax",
"LineItems": [{
"Description": "Forgot to cancel annual sub payment",
"LineAmount": "100.00"  }]
}
```

--------------------------------

### Add Note Request Body

Source: https://developer.xero.com/documentation/api/accounting/banktransfers

Example JSON structure for adding a note to a bank transfer. The 'Details' field within 'HistoryRecords' specifies the content of the note.

```JSON
{
"HistoryRecords": [
    {
"Details": "Note added by your favourite app!"    }
    ...
  ]
}
```

--------------------------------

### Filter Accounts by EnablePaymentsToAccount

Source: https://developer.xero.com/documentation/guides/how-to-guides/integration-best-practices

This example shows how to filter the list of accounts retrieved from Xero. It specifically looks for accounts where 'EnablePaymentsToAccount' is true and the 'AccountType' is 'BANK', which is necessary for handling payments correctly.

```JavaScript
function filterAccountsForPayments(accounts) {
  return accounts.filter(account => 
    account.EnablePaymentsToAccount && account.AccountType === 'BANK'
  );
}
```

--------------------------------

### Single BankTransaction Response (Xero API)

Source: https://developer.xero.com/documentation/api/accounting/banktransactions

This is an example JSON response when retrieving a single BankTransaction from the Xero API. It details the transaction's attributes, including associated contact, bank account, and line item information.

```JSON
{
"BankTransactions": [{
"Contact": {
"ContactID": "6d42f03b-181f-43e3-93fb-2025c012de92",
"Name": "Wilson Periodicals"
},
"DateString": "2014-05-26T00:00:00",
"Date": "\/Date(1401062400000+0000)\/",
"Status": "AUTHORISED",
"LineAmountTypes": "Inclusive",
"LineItems": [{
"Description": "Monthly account fee",
"UnitAmount": "49.90",
"TaxType": "NONE",
"TaxAmount": "0.00",
"LineAmount": "49.90",
"AccountCode": "404",
"Quantity": "1.0000",
"LineItemID": "52208ff9-528a-4985-a9ad-b2b1d4210e38"
}],
"SubTotal": "49.90",
"TotalTax": "0.00",
"Total": "49.90",
"UpdatedDateUTC": "\/Date(1439434356790+0000)\/",
"CurrencyCode": "NZD",
"BankTransactionID": "d20b6c54-7f5d-4ce6-ab83-55f609719126",
"BankAccount": {
"AccountID": "ac993f75-035b-433c-82e0-7b7a2d40802c",
"Code": "090",
"Name": "Business Bank Account"
},
"BatchPayment": {
"Account": {
"AccountID": "ac993f75-035b-433c-82e0-7b7a2d40802c"
},
"BatchPaymentID": "b54aa50c-794c-461b-89d1-846e1b84d9c0",
"Date": "\/Date(1401062400000+0000)\/",
"Type": "RECBATCH",
"Status": "AUTHORISED",
"TotalAmount": "100.00",
"UpdatedDateUTC": "\/Date(1439434356790+0000)\/",
"IsReconciled": "true"
},
"Type": "SPEND",
"Reference": "Sub 098801",
"IsReconciled": "true"
}]
}
```

--------------------------------

### Create Bank Transaction (Spend Money)

Source: https://developer.xero.com/documentation/api/accounting/banktransactions

Example of creating a 'Spend Money' bank transaction. Requires mandatory fields like Type, Contact, Lineitems, and BankAccount. Lineitems include Description, Quantity, UnitAmount, and AccountCode.

```JSON
{
  "Type": "SPEND",
  "Contact": {
    "ContactID": "3fa85f64-5717-4562-b3fc-2c963f66afa6"
  },
  "LineItems": [
    {
      "Description": "Office Supplies",
      "Quantity": 1,
      "UnitAmount": 50.00,
      "AccountCode": "495"
    }
  ],
  "BankAccount": {
    "AccountID": "f44718c1-736c-4303-a719-204102207177"
  }
}
```
